heading: Yengo National Park vs Yennora — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Yengo National Park, New South Wales and Yennora, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Yengo National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Yennora (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Yengo National Park or Yennora safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Yengo National Park has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Yennora scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Yengo National Park scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Yennora scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Yengo National Park or Yennora?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Yengo National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
